introduction: This presentation provides a detailed review of Elexion Automotive's investment in charging infrastructure partnerships and installations across North America in 2024. The insights will aid in understanding our strategic financial positioning and future planning.

subsections.heading: EV Market Trends and Charging Infrastructure
subsections.content:
• The global EV market is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 26% through 2026
• In 2024, the total number of EV charging stations in the US exceeded 100,000
• Public charging infrastructure remains a challenge, with less than one public charger for every ten EVs on the road in 2024
• The North American EV market is expected to reach $180 billion by 2027
• In 2023, the US government approved a $7.5 billion investment in EV charging infrastructure
• Elexion's home charging solution, Elexion Charge, was launched in 2019
subsections.justification: These points discuss industry trends and Elexion’s product launch, but not Elexion’s specific investments in charging infrastructure in 2024.

subsections.heading: Elexion's 2024 Charging Infrastructure Investments
subsections.content:
• Elexion’s ‘Charge Ahead’ program aimed to accelerate the deployment of charging infrastructure
• In Q2 2024, Elexion expanded its charging network to 3 new states
• The company increased its charging stations by 30% in 2024
• Elexion’s R&D team developed a new fast-charging technology in 2024
• The 'Charge Ahead' program was primarily funded through a mix of private investments and public grants
subsections.justification: These points discuss Elexion’s charging infrastructure initiatives and growth, but do not specify the total investment amount in 2024.

subsections.heading: Partnerships and Collaborations for Charging Infrastructure
subsections.content:
• Elexion partnered with GridPower in 2023 to enhance its charging technology
• In 2024, Elexion collaborated with CityCharge to install EV charging stations in urban areas
• Elexion joined the Charge America coalition to advocate for more investment in charging infrastructure
• The collaboration with state governments assisted in the expansion of Elexion’s charging network
• Discussions with European charging manufacturer, LightningCharge, began in late 2024
subsections.justification: These points mention partnerships and collaborations, but do not disclose the specific financial investments involved in these activities in 2024.

conclusion: Elexion's strategic investments in charging infrastructure and partnerships in 2024 reflected our commitment to driving the transition to zero-emission transportation. Moving forward, we will continue to evaluate and seize opportunities that align with our sustainability vision and the evolving EV market trends.
